reason: Split generateHandoffSystem/handoffReviewSystem into *Base consts + generateHandoffSystemPrompt(language?)/handoffReviewSystemPrompt(language?) builders via a shared withLanguage(prompt, language?) helper that appends '\nWrite your entire response in {language}.' only when language?.trim() is non-empty (else byte-identical). W6 directive reaches BOTH phases so the reviewer does not penalize a non-English draft.

GenerateHandoffInput.language?: string; destructured + Phase-1 system uses generateHandoffSystemPrompt(language); reviewDraft gains language and uses handoffReviewSystemPrompt(args.language). Cache/force/result-shape/trace paths unchanged; language independent of force.

Accept optional language?: string from request.data, validate typeof==='string' when defined, pass into generateHandoffFlow({...,force:true,runId,language}). runId validation + force semantics untouched.

summarizeDaySystem -> summarizeDaySystemBase + summarizeDaySystemPrompt(language?) appending the single conditional line; deterministic counts/contributions prompt-free (computed in TS) so they stay language-neutral.
SummarizeDayInput.language?: string -> runReportAgent(...,language) -> narrative system prompt via summarizeDaySystemPrompt(language). Step-1 deterministic context untouched.
Accept + validate optional language?: string, pass into summarizeDayFlow. date/range normalization + caps unchanged.
explainCommitSystem -> explainCommitSystemBase + explainCommitSystemPrompt(language?) appending the single conditional line.

ExplainCommitInput.language?: string; doc path AND explainFromGitHub fallback path both use explainCommitSystemPrompt(language). Cache (force) short-circuit, not-found guard, best-effort write-back unchanged.
Accept + validate optional language?: string, pass into explainCommitFlow. owner/repo/token GitHub-fallback resolution unchanged.
AppLocaleX.backendLanguage tiny map (en->English, zhHant->Traditional Chinese) — the W6 locale->name single source of truth (English NAME convention).
AppStrings.backendLanguage => locale.backendLanguage, so call sites read it via the fallback-safe context.l10n accessor used everywhere else.
generateHandoff/summarizeDay/explainCommit abstract + _LiveFunctionsService gain optional String? language, sent via the 'language': ?language conditional-map-entry shorthand (only when set).
Same optional language signatures; when set, canned handoff/summary/explanation are annotated (Regenerated/Recomputed in {language} - fake demo) so a regenerate is visibly different in fake mode.
regenerate({String? language}) + generateDay(day,{String? language}) forward language to summarizeDay.
explain(sha,{force,language}) forwards language to explainCommit (sent on recompute/force).

Per-day Generate/Regenerate buttons pass context.l10n.backendLanguage to generateDay; commit-detail recompute IconButton passes s.backendLanguage with force:true. First-tap auto explain (vm.explain(sha)) left language-free = default path.
Existing handoff regenerate (_regenerateHandoff, force=true) wired to pass s.backendLanguage. No new button needed — a force/regenerate action already existed.
